name: Natural Reserve Lagunas de Villafáfila — circular by Villarrín de Campos
name_original: Reserva Natural Lagunas de Villafáfila — circular por Villarrín de Campos
region_intro: Mountain biking around Revellinos offers routes through a distinctive natural landscape, primarily centered around the Villafáfila Lagoons Nature Reserve. This area is characterized by expansive natural lagoons and a wetland ecosystem, providing a flat, open terrain for cycling. The trails are generally well-trodden, offering extensive views and opportunities for wildlife observation, particularly birdlife. While much of the landscape is flat, some sections can present varied terrain, especially when wet. Best mountain bike trails around Revellinos The most popular mountain bike trail is Ruta de la Plata Greenway Trail, a 14.2 miles (22.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete. This easy route follows a historic greenway, offering a smooth and accessible ride. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Reserva Natural de Lagunas de Villafáfila — ruta circular, a moderate 25.3 miles (40.8 km) path. This circular route explores the heart of the nature reserve, passing by significant lagoons. Local mountain bikers also love the Natural Reserve Lagunas de Villafáfila — circular by Villarrín de Campos, a 21.4 miles (34.4 km) trail leading through the natural reserve and past Villarrín de Campos, often completed in about 2 hours 26 minutes. Mountain biking around Revellinos is defined by flat, open landscapes, the extensive Villafáfila Lagoons Nature Reserve, and well-trodden trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy greenways to moderate circular routes. The routes in Revellinos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 4 reviews. More than 20 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Revellinos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Revellinos?
A: There are 5 mountain bike trails available around Revellinos. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from easy greenways to moderate circular paths through the natural reserve.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Revellinos?
A: The mountain bike trails in Revellinos are generally considered easy to moderate. There are 2 easy routes and 3 moderate routes, with no difficult trails listed. The terrain is often flat and well-trodden, making it accessible for various skill levels, though some sections can become trickier when wet.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Revellinos?
A: Yes, Revellinos offers easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232518044/ruta-de-la-plata-greenway-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de la Plata Greenway Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 14.2 miles (22.9 km) long and follows a historic greenway, providing a smooth and accessible ride.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes in Revellinos?
A: Yes, you'll find several circular mountain bike routes in Revellinos. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018926811/reserva-natural-de-lagunas-de-villafafila-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reserva Natural de Lagunas de Villafáfila — ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 25.3 miles (40.8 km) path that explores the heart of the nature reserve. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309720607/natural-reserve-lagunas-de-villafafila-circular-by-villarrin-de-campos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Reserve Lagunas de Villafáfila — circular by Villarrín de Campos&lt;/a&gt;, a 21.4 miles (34.4 km) trail.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails in Revellinos?
A: The trails around Revellinos offer access to the unique natural landscapes of the Villafáfila Lagoons Nature Reserve. You can observe significant birdlife, especially around the natural lagoons like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salina Grande Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6133740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, you might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Otero de Sariegos Bird Observatory&lt;/a&gt; and historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438063" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Villarigo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Revellinos?
A: The Villafáfila Lagoons Nature Reserve is a significant bird sanctuary, especially during winter and spring. While these seasons offer excellent wildlife viewing, some trails, particularly around Laguna de San Pedro, can become tricky for mountain bikes when wet. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and drier conditions, making them ideal for cycling.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails around Revellinos suitable for families?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Revellinos are suitable for families, particularly the easy routes. The flat, well-trodden paths and the scenic, open landscapes of the Villafáfila Lagoons Nature Reserve provide a relaxed and enjoyable experience for all skill levels. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232518044/ruta-de-la-plata-greenway-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de la Plata Greenway Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent family-friendly option.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Revellinos?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 4 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the quiet, open spaces, the extensive views, and the unique opportunity to observe wildlife, especially the diverse birdlife within the Villafáfila Lagoons Nature Reserve.

Q: Are there any longer mountain bike routes for more experienced riders?
A: For riders looking for longer routes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018926811/reserva-natural-de-lagunas-de-villafafila-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reserva Natural de Lagunas de Villafáfila — ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; covers 25.3 miles (40.8 km), and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021514935/rio-esla-and-via-de-la-plata-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Esla and Vía de la Plata Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is 22.6 miles (36.3 km), both offering moderate challenges and extended riding through the region's distinctive landscapes.

Q: Can I expect to see wildlife while mountain biking in Revellinos?
A: Absolutely. Revellinos is situated near the Villafáfila Lagoons Nature Reserve, a significant bird sanctuary. Cyclists often have the chance to observe a variety of birdlife, including geese, ducks, and waders, especially during winter and spring. The quiet, open spaces enhance the wildlife observation experience.

Q: Are there any routes that combine natural scenery with historical points of interest?
A: Yes, routes like the circular trail that passes by &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salina Grande Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Otero de Sariegos Bird Observatory&lt;/a&gt;, and the Roman bridge in Villafáfila offer a blend of nature, history, and physical activity. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021514935/rio-esla-and-via-de-la-plata-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Esla and Vía de la Plata Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; also follows a historic path.
tips:
Laguna Salina Grande is located in the Villafáfila Nature Reserve, in the province of Zamora. This lagoon is the largest in the reserve and is an ideal place for birdwatching, especially during winter and spring. In winter, thousands of geese and ducks congregate there, while in spring, a wide variety of waders can be observed.

One of the most interesting routes to explore the area is the circular trail that passes by Laguna Salina Grande, the Otero de Sariegos bird observatory, and the Roman bridge in Villafáfila. This route offers an excellent opportunity to enjoy the reserve's wildlife and landscape.

This entire area is a wildlife sanctuary and a great place for bird watching. It's a great place to visit after the rainy season to see more birds, but when wet the trails can be tricky for a mountain bike.
Abandoned town dedicated to the production of salt from the dry bed lagoons. It is now a tourist attraction for those bird lovers who visit during the migration season.
